pattern: Precomputing how many consecutive ones extend leftward and upward from each cell lets every candidate square's four-sided border be validated in constant time.
hints: A square's border is valid when its top and bottom rows are full of ones and its left and right columns are full of ones.
Precompute, for each cell, the run length of ones reaching to its left and the run reaching upward; these answer 'is this segment all ones?' instantly.
Scan candidate squares from the largest side down; the bottom-right corner needs left/up runs >= side, and the two opposite corners must reach back across the side too.

edge_inputs.description: solid interior but largest valid border is smaller square

stress.description: random binary grids biased toward ones
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    r = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 30)))
    c = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 30)))
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.7 else 0 for _ in range(c)] for _ in range(r)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: dense ones produce frequent borders to validate
